title: selinum neglectum
intro: Selinum neglectum, also known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a biennial herbaceous plant in the carrot family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Selinum neglectum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the roots and seeds often being used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
